text: We introduce a novel dataset designed to advance the state-of-the-art in object recognition by embedding it within the broader challenge of scene understanding. Rather than focusing on isolated items, we gathered images of complex, everyday scenes that depict common objects in their natural environments. To support precise localization, we labeled these objects using per-instance segmentations. The dataset features 91 object categories—all of which are easily recognizable by a four-year-old child—and encompasses 2.5 million labeled instances across 328,000 images. Building this resource required extensive crowdsourcing facilitated by innovative user interfaces tailored for category detection, instance spotting, and segmentation. We provide a comprehensive statistical comparison of our dataset against PASCAL, ImageNet, and SUN, and establish performance baselines for bounding box and segmentation tasks using a Deformable Parts Model.
